summary: The session focused on the demographic breakdown of Saginaw's population, including age and ethnic diversity, based on data from the 2010 United States Census. The user inquired about changes in demographics since 2010 and sought more information on the ethnic composition of the city.
facts: The median age in Saginaw was 31.9 years according to the 2010 United States Census. There were 16,667 residents under the age of 18 in Saginaw as of the 2010 Census. There were 4,017 residents over the age of 65 in Saginaw as of the 2010 Census. Saginaw has a population of approximately 48,000 residents according to the US Census Bureau. The largest racial group in Saginaw is white, comprising 34.3% of the population. African American or Black residents make up 57.4% of Saginaw's population. Hispanic or Latino residents constitute 4.4% of Saginaw's population. The American Indian and Alaska Native population in Saginaw is 0.4%. Asian residents comprise 0.8% of Saginaw's population. Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander residents account for 0.1% of Saginaw's population. 2.6% of Saginaw's population identifies as two or more races or ethnicities.
